title: Othocallis cilicica
taxonomy: Othocallis cilicica is classified within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. This family comprises approximately 1,900 to 2,100 species of flowering plants. The genus Othocallis itself is a small genus of flowering plants, with Othocallis cilicica being one of its few known species. The classification of Othocallis cilicica is still a topic of ongoing research, and more information may be discovered as studies continue. Further classification details may be found within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta.
constituents: Othocallis cilicica is a plant species that contains a range of active constituents, although specific information is limited. However, its close relative, Othocallis cilicica  is reported to contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have been identified in other members of the same family, Rutaceae. Alkaloids, which are typically found in plants that belong to the Rutaceae family, have been reported to exhibit various biological activities. Further research is needed to confirm the specific active constituents present in Othocallis cilicica.
parts: Othocallis cilicica, also known as the Turkish primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the primrose family. Unfortunately, there is limited information available about its medicinal parts. However, the plant's flowers and leaves are known to be used in traditional medicine in Turkey. Further research is needed to confirm the specific medicinal properties and parts of the plant. More studies are necessary to fully understand its potential benefits and uses.
intro: Othocallis cilicica, also known as Turkish angelica, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), characterized by its tall stems, feathery leaves, and umbrella-shaped inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which provide relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Othocallis cilicica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Turkish angelica is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
